texto: Code is not like other how-computers-work books. It doesn't have big color illustrations of disk drives with arrows showing how the data sweeps into the computer. Code has no drawings of trains carrying a cargo of zeros and ones. Metaphors and similes are wonderful literary devices but they do nothing but obscure the beauty of technology.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charles Petzold: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Code is not like other how-computers-work books. It doesn't have big color illustrations of disk …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, code, computer-science pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e trabalho.
